exemplar.title: How can mathematics, particularly Voronoi diagrams, Euclidean distance, statistical analysis, and Linear Programming can be used to determine the most strategic location for a burger stall in a town?

subject.title: Mathematics Applications & Interpretation (AI)
subject.description:
**IB Mathematics: Applications and Interpretation** is a **Group 5** subject in the **International Baccalaureate Diploma Programme (IBDP)**. It explores **real-world applications of mathematics, statistical analysis, mathematical modeling, and data interpretation**, with a strong focus on **technology use, practical problem-solving, and interpreting mathematical results in context**.

The course is available at **Standard Level (SL, 150 teaching hours)** and **Higher Level (HL, 240 teaching hours)**, with **first assessment in 2021**.

### Assessment at a Glance

| Assessment | Format | SL | HL | Weighting |
|------------|--------|----|----|-----------|
| **Paper 1** | External examination (calculator permitted) | 1h 30min | 2h | SL: 40%, HL: 30% |
| **Paper 2** | External examination (technology required) | 1h 30min | 2h | SL: 40%, HL: 30% |
| **Paper 3** | External examination (technology required) | N/A | 1h | HL: 20% |
| **Mathematical Exploration** | Internal assessment | 30h | 30h | 20% |

strengths: Clear overall structure with a logical flow from introduction through methodology to conclusion
Effective use of headings, sub-headings and visuals to guide the reader
Coherent development of the argument with minimal digressions
weaknesses: Occasional repetition of background narrative detracts from focus
Some page-layout and formatting inconsistencies (bullet styles, broken sentences)
Conclusion section ends abruptly without a full synthesis of findings
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Strand assessments placed two areas at band 3 (coherence & organisation) and one at band 2 (conciseness). On balance, the work is well organised and largely coherent, warranting a 3/4.

strengths: Effective use of multiple representations (tables, GeoGebra screenshots, hand-drawn graphs) to illuminate concepts
Generally clear explanations of steps, making the mathematics accessible
Most notation is appropriate and consistent in context
weaknesses: Inconsistent unit notation and occasional misplaced variable definitions
Minor typographical errors in list labels and Greek symbols
Some diagrams lack axis labels or resolution for full clarity
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Although notation and clarity occasionally lapse (band 2), the studentâs effective multi-modal presentation and mostly consistent mathematical language (band 3) justify a solid 3/4.

strengths: Strong personal narrative that clearly motivated choice of context
Evidence of independent thinking in selecting and combining four HL-level tools
First-person commentary shows genuine engagement beyond a template approach
weaknesses: Creativity is moderate; methods follow standard textbook patterns without novel adaptation
Limited exploration of alternative personal strategies or âwhat-ifâ scenarios
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Independent thinking and personal approach are both at band 2, while creativity sits at band 1. Overall this balances to a 2/3.

strengths: Includes short reflective comments at the end of each section
weaknesses: Reflection is brief and descriptive rather than analytical
Critical analysis lacks depthâno discussion of statistical assumptions or method robustness
Connections between mathematical findings and real-world decisionâmaking are minimal
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: All three strands fall solidly into band 1, demonstrating only limited and basic reflection. A score of 1/3 is appropriate.

strengths: Applies a range of HL-level mathematics (Voronoi geometry, Euclidean metrics, one-sample t-test, linear programming)
Integrates multiple advanced tools into a coherent modelling framework
Demonstrates sophisticated understanding by interpreting results within the business context
weaknesses: Minor errors in unit labelling and rounding inconsistencies
Some coordinates and constraints are read graphically rather than derived algebraically
Limited discussion of underlying statistical and LP assumptions
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The exploration uses multiple high-level mathematical techniques in a rigorous modelling context and shows deep conceptual understanding. Despite minor slips, the overall sophistication and breadth merit the top level, 6/6.

strandSelections.reasoning: Selecting a personally meaningful context, gathering (albeit hypothetical) data, and deciding to combine four mathematical techniques show significant independent thinking. Choices are explained in the first person and go beyond a textbook example, satisfying the descriptor for âsignificant evidenceâ.

Explanations show good conceptual graspâVoronoi regions are interpreted, the rationale for a one-sample t-test is stated, limitations are recognised. Yet there is limited discussion of underlying assumptions or robustness, keeping understanding at a âgood in partsâ rather than thorough or rigorous level.

Distance mis-labelling (metres vs kilometres), an incorrect coordinate in the Euclidean example, and rounding inconsistencies in the linear-programming vertices indicate noticeable errors. However, key calculations (t-value, feasible region) are substantially correct, so the work is âpartially correctâ rather than âmostly correctâ.

While most content is relevant to the research question, sizeable portions of anecdotal description and general fast-food commentary do not drive the mathematical investigation. Definitions already stated are restated later, and the limitations section repeats earlier caveats. Thus the write-up is âmostly relevant with some unnecessary elementsâ, matching band 2.

Standard notation (Voronoi cell, Euclidean formula, linear inequalities) is used, yet symbols are sometimes mis-matched with text (e.g. 5 m versus 9 km, inconsistent bold type, missing units). Variable definitions for x and y appear only after they are used, and the null/alternative hypotheses are described partly in words. This constitutes âsome appropriate useâ rather than âmostly appropriateâ.

Most steps are explained clearly, yet occasional notational slips (Greek letters rendered in plain text, mis-ordered list labels) and the earlier distance inconsistency reduce overall consistency. The communication therefore falls into âmostly clear with some consistencyâ rather than the higher category.

All mathematics usedâVoronoi geometry, Euclidean metrics, one-sample t-test, linear programmingâsit squarely within HL AI syllabus and are relevant to answering the research question. They are applied in appropriate contexts, though not always to maximum depth, matching the descriptor ârelevant mathematics at course level usedâ.

Tables, labelled GeoGebra screenshots, a hand-drawn feasible region, and algebraic expressions provide multiple avenues for the reader to understand the work. Each representation corresponds sensibly to the context being discussed. Minor issues with resolution and scale prevent it from being called âsophisticatedâ, but their use is certainly effective.

Reflection existsâeach section ends with short comments on what was learnedâbut the discussion is brief and descriptive. It seldom probes deeper implications or evaluates robustness in depth. Hence the work demonstrates âlimited reflectionâ.

Using Voronoi diagrams and linear programming in a food-stall scenario is moderately original, but the mathematical execution stays close to standard textbook methods. Little innovative modelling or novel interpretation is attempted, so evidence of creativity is present but limited.

The exploration is arranged in a clear sequence: introduction, background study, separate sections for each mathematical tool, limitations and conclusion. Although transitions can occasionally feel abrupt, the logical thread of âidentify location â test accessibility â test demand â test profitâ is easy to follow and rarely breaks. Isolated language errors do not seriously impede the line of argument, so overall coherence and development are judged to be solid rather than outstanding.

The critical analysis of results is basic: the student states that Burger X is optimal because of higher traffic or larger profit without examining sensitivity or alternative models. The critique of limitations is largely generic, aligning with âbasic analysis with limited critiqueâ.

Links between mathematical outcomes and enhanced understanding of the business problem are claimed but not convincingly elaborated; for instance, no concrete decisionâmaking thresholds or future recommendations are provided. The connection therefore remains limited.

Headings and sub-headings structure the work effectively; tables, figures and appendices are placed in sensible positions. The reader can locate objectives, methodology, results and conclusion without effort. Some repetition (e.g. fast-food narrative reappearing) and occasional page-layout problems stop the report from feeling âexceptionallyâ smooth, but the organisation clearly meets the âwell-organisedâ descriptor.

The studentâs narrative links childhood experience to the decision process, and personal commentary accompanies each mathematical stage. This personalised drive steers the exploration rather than a teacher-given template, matching a âclear personal approachâ.

annotations.comment: The title effectively conveys the focus on strategic location but could be more precise by specifying the mathematical tools to be explored.

The research question repeats âcanâ twice in its phrasing, making it slightly awkward. Consider rewording for clarity: âHow can Voronoi diagrams, distance metrics, statistical analysis and linear programming be usedâ¦â
The anecdotal introduction is engaging but contains extensive narrative that distracts from the mathematical purpose. Trim personal story to focus on justification for the chosen methods.
The second paragraph provides useful context about fast food but includes irrelevant examples. Prioritize content that directly motivates the mathematical investigation.
The term âweatherâ in objectives is a spelling errorâit should read âwhetherâ. Proofread to correct minor language inaccuracies.
Methodology outlines each tool but does not explain how their results will be integrated. Add a statement on combining findings to justify the final recommendation.
Background study focuses on general business location decisions but could be condensed. Replace broad business advice with a concise link to your mathematical approach.
The objectives are presented in a paragraph. Converting these to a numbered list would improve clarity and highlight each investigative aim.
All distance values lack unit notation in their results (e.g., 4.47 should read 4.47 units). Append units to each computed distance for mathematical precision.
The null and alternative hypotheses are described in words but not formatted as $$H_0$$ and $$H_1$$. Use proper notation to strengthen mathematical communication.
Sample size (n=10 hours) for the t-test is small. Reflect on how this may affect the validity and power of your hypothesis test.
When selecting the critical value for a one-tailed t-test, clarify that the table provides two-tailed values and adjust if necessary for one-tailed analysis.
The linear programming introduction breaks mid-sentence. Complete the definition of linear optimization to improve readability.
In stating the objective function $$P=2x+1.5y$$, explicitly indicate that x and y represent cheeseburgers and beef burgers respectively.
Each constraint inequality is listed but not labeled. Number or letter each constraint to facilitate clear referencing in the solution process.
The feasible region is described descriptively, but the coordinates of intersections should be derived algebraically rather than read from the graph.
The list of vertices uses inconsistent bullet symbols and formatting. Standardize list formatting for professional presentation.
The limitation section identifies weaknesses of methods but lacks deeper critique of which tool is most robust in this context. Consider ranking methods by reliability.
Conclusion ends abruptly and transitions into appendices without a clear wrap-up. Ensure the conclusions section fully synthesizes findings.
The stray expression $$\sqrt{\frac{a}{\sqrt{a}}}$$ in the appendix is irrelevant to the exploration. Remove or replace with relevant content.
The historical note on Voronoi diagrams is interesting but delays application. Move or shorten history to focus sooner on your specific spatial analysis.
In Table 1 the coordinate system and units are not defined. Specify whether these are grid units representing kilometers or another scale.
Figure 1 appears pixelated and its axes lack labels. Improve image resolution and add axis titles to communicate spatial relationships clearly.
The statement that each distance is âapproximately 9 kmâ contradicts earlier mention of meters. Ensure consistent units throughout the Euclidean distance discussion.
When introducing the formula $$d=\sqrt{(x_1-x_2)^2+(y_1-y_2)^2}$$, explicitly define each variable in relation to your foot traffic coordinates.
The foot traffic coordinate table lacks units and scale. Clarify whether these are grid points, meters, or another measurement to avoid ambiguity.
The mis-formatting of the list label âiii.â as â\dddot{m}â is a typographic error that reduces clarity. Correct list labels for consistency.
The assumption of normality for foot traffic data is not discussed. Include a brief critique of statistical assumptions to demonstrate critical analysis.
References are listed as URLs without a consistent citation style. Adopt a formal referencing format (e.g., APA or MLA) for academic rigor.
The studentâs personal narrative throughout demonstrates strong engagement and authentic motivation, enriching the exploration.
